about.heading: Healing Rooted in Faith
about.body: I'm a Licensed Clinical Social Worker based in Colorado with nearly a decade of experience in the mental health field, including six years of post-graduate clinical practice. I provide private-pay therapy for individuals ages 17 and older, as well as family therapy when healing requires more than one voice in the room.
about.body2: As a practicing Catholic, I also welcome clients who desire thoughtful integration of faith into therapy. Faith is never assumed and always client-led, but when desired, therapy can be a space where psychological insight and spiritual truth work together in the healing process.
about.body3: I intentionally maintain a small, private-pay practice so I can offer focused, personalized care. My hope is that therapy with me feels steady, honest, and deeply human — a place where what feels heavy can be gently untangled and what feels true can grow stronger.

services.name: Individual Therapy
services.description: One-on-one sessions for ages 17 and older addressing depression, anxiety, OCD, perfectionism, ADHD, trauma, and relationship challenges. Drawing from IFS, CBT, DBT, and attachment-based frameworks.

services.name: Family Therapy
services.description: When healing requires more than one voice in the room. Family sessions focused on attachment patterns, family-of-origin dynamics, and building stronger connections.

pricing.name: Initial Consultation
pricing.description: A brief introductory call to discuss your needs and determine if we are a good fit.

pricing.name: Individual Session
pricing.description: Standard individual counseling session with a licensed counselor. Sliding scale available based on need.

counselor.name: Katie Clemente, LCSW
counselor.title: Founder & Licensed Clinical Social Worker
counselor.bio: I specialize in working with depression, anxiety, OCD, perfectionism, ADHD, trauma, and relationship challenges. Much of my work involves family-of-origin exploration, attachment patterns, and the ways early experiences continue to shape present struggles. I believe many symptoms make sense in context — and therapy is a space to approach those patterns with curiosity rather than shame.
counselor.bio2: My approach is trauma-informed, relational, and depth-oriented. I draw from Internal Family Systems (IFS), Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT), and attachment-based frameworks. I especially value IFS work, which helps clients understand the different "parts" of themselves — the anxious part, the overachieving part, the critical part — and move toward greater internal clarity and integration.
counselor.bio3: I work often with high-capacity, deeply feeling individuals who are used to holding it together for everyone else. If you struggle with intrusive thoughts, emotional intensity, over-responsibility, or feeling "too much" or "not enough," you are not alone. Healing doesn't require becoming someone new — it often involves coming home to yourself with steadiness and compassion.

footer.quote: Remember, O most gracious Virgin Mary, that never was it known that anyone who fled to thy protection, implored thy help, or sought thine intercession was left unaided.
